evidence: Continuous capability prose — 'manufactured to your specification' — not a SKU list.
Size stated as a continuous span (#0000 → 1.500 in / M0.8 → M39), with no enumerated part numbers.
Thread forms listed are standard and licensed profiles (UN, ISO, Acme, plus named third-party systems) — capabilities, not products.
Buyer specifies a requirement (target part, thread form, material, tolerance) and requests a quote.

viability.notes: No discrete product table — a guided configurator is the right surface. Specification axes are clear, but constraint logic between thread form, material, and tolerance is non-trivial, so build complexity is high.

about.blurb: Precision thread-rolling and form tooling, made to order for fastener, aerospace, and medical manufacturing.

specAxes.label: Thread size range
specAxes.helpText: Metric M0.8–M39 or imperial #0000–1.500 in. State the target nominal diameter.
specAxes.constraints: Diameters below M1 require a fine-pitch capability review before quoting.

specAxes.label: Thread form
specAxes.helpText: Standard and licensed profiles are supported. Licensed third-party thread systems are capabilities, not catalogue products.
specAxes.constraints: Some aerospace profiles require customer-supplied licensing documentation.

specAxes.label: Workpiece material
specAxes.helpText: Drives die hardness, coating, and expected tool life.

specAxes.label: Target part type
specAxes.helpText: What you will produce on the finished tool.

specAxes.label: Tolerance class
specAxes.helpText: Tighter classes increase die grind and inspection time.

specAxes.label: Annual volume
specAxes.helpText: Drives die material and coating selection for wear life.

capabilities.title: Cylindrical & flat-die thread rolling
capabilities.body: Rolled threads for superior fatigue life and grain flow versus cut threads, from miniature to heavy fasteners.

capabilities.title: Thread & profile grinding
capabilities.body: Ground dies and rolls for tight tolerance classes and complex or non-standard forms.

capabilities.title: In-house heat treat & coating
capabilities.body: NADCAP heat treat plus nitride and PVD coatings tuned to the workpiece material for tool life.

capabilities.title: Reverse-engineering & sample matching
capabilities.body: Submit a sample or a worn tool; we reverse the profile and reproduce or improve it.

technicalLibrary.name: Thread rolling vs cutting — selection guide
technicalLibrary.note: When rolling wins on fatigue life, material savings, and surface finish.

technicalLibrary.name: Workpiece hardness vs die-life chart
technicalLibrary.note: Expected tool life bands by material hardness and coating.

technicalLibrary.name: ISO 5408 thread terminology
technicalLibrary.note: Terminology used throughout our quotations and drawings.

technicalLibrary.name: DXF / STEP submission template
technicalLibrary.note: How to submit a target thread profile for quotation.

confidenceFlags.message: Thread-form names include licensed and third-party systems. These are CAPABILITIES, not products — they must never be scraped into a SKU list. Excluded accordingly.
Size is a continuous span (#0000 → 1.500 in / M0.8 → M39); recorded as a range rather than enumerated part numbers.
Capability-led brochure with no discrete SKU table — classified as a configurator. Catalogue fit is weak; do not force a product grid.
